explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic changed my work and personal life dramatically starting in 2020. As a dietitian working in clinical and public health settings, I shifted from seeing patients in person to doing mostly telephone appointments. This made it harder to communicate effectively, especially with non-English speaking clients, and I faced challenges with interpreter availability and lack of visual tools. Personally, the pandemic also impacted me deeply as I became a grandmother during this time but was limited in visiting my granddaughter. We relied heavily on Zoom and FaceTime to stay connected during the isolation.
implicit_text: The global health crisis that began in 2020 changed my work and personal life dramatically. As a dietitian working in clinical and public health settings, I shifted from seeing patients in person to doing mostly telephone appointments. This made it harder to communicate effectively, especially with non-English speaking clients, and I faced challenges with interpreter availability and lack of visual tools. Personally, this period also impacted me deeply as I became a grandmother during this time but was limited in visiting my granddaughter. We relied heavily on video calling apps to stay connected during the isolation.

explicit_text: I have been working through the WIC clinic as part of my role as a dietitian for many years. During the COVID-19 pandemic, WIC visits changed significantly because many certification visits were waived and appointments moved to telephone. This made it difficult to monitor high-risk patients, especially infants and breastfeeding mothers, since I couldn't weigh or visually assess them. Many clients struggled to understand how to use their benefits over the phone, and I had to spend extra time explaining processes and mailing handouts. Despite these challenges, the state was very supportive in allowing some staff to work from home to continue WIC services.
implicit_text: I have been working through the government-supported nutrition assistance program for women, infants, and children as part of my role as a dietitian for many years. During the recent health crisis, visits through this program changed significantly because many certification visits were waived and appointments moved to telephone. This made it difficult to monitor high-risk patients, especially infants and breastfeeding mothers, since I couldn't weigh or visually assess them. Many clients struggled to understand how to use their benefits over the phone, and I had to spend extra time explaining processes and mailing handouts. Despite these challenges, the state was very supportive in allowing some staff to work from home to continue these services.

explicit_text: I work as a dietitian in California, where I have been serving clinical and public health patients for nearly three decades. During the COVID-19 pandemic, California's WIC program adapted by allowing remote work and telephone appointments to continue serving the community. The local community here was very supportive, often providing meals to essential workers like myself during the difficult months of the shutdown. The environment in my workplace in California became quieter and less staffed due to furloughs, but we remained cautious and caring towards each other.
implicit_text: I work as a dietitian in the most populous state on the West Coast, where I have been serving clinical and public health patients for nearly three decades. During the recent health crisis, the state's nutrition assistance program adapted by allowing remote work and telephone appointments to continue serving the community. The local community here was very supportive, often providing meals to essential workers like myself during the difficult months of the shutdown. The environment in my workplace in this state became quieter and less staffed due to furloughs, but we remained cautious and caring towards each other.

explicit_text: During the COVID-19 pandemic, I learned to appreciate Zoom Video Communications as a vital tool for staying connected. Since I couldn't visit my granddaughter in person due to safety concerns, we relied on Zoom for virtual visits. It helped me maintain relationships with family and also facilitated some work communication when in-person meetings were not possible. Zoom became an essential part of my daily life during the isolation periods of 2020.
implicit_text: During the recent health crisis, I learned to appreciate a popular video conferencing platform as a vital tool for staying connected. Since I couldn't visit my granddaughter in person due to safety concerns, we relied on this platform for virtual visits. It helped me maintain relationships with family and also facilitated some work communication when in-person meetings were not possible. This tool became an essential part of my daily life during the isolation periods of 2020.

explicit_text: FaceTime was another important technology that helped me during the COVID-19 pandemic. Since I was unable to see my granddaughter and family in person, FaceTime allowed us to have video calls and feel closer despite the distance. It became a regular part of how I stayed connected and supported my loved ones through the isolation. I really came to appreciate this tool as a way to bridge the gap during a very challenging time.
implicit_text: A well-known video calling feature on Apple devices was another important technology that helped me during the recent health crisis. Since I was unable to see my granddaughter and family in person, this app allowed us to have video calls and feel closer despite the distance. It became a regular part of how I stayed connected and supported my loved ones through the isolation. I really came to appreciate this tool as a way to bridge the gap during a very challenging time.
